what_it_does: Acceptance test for a file-discovered mark-type registry: it discovers seed mark-types from the mark_types directory, validates dynamic add/remove of mark-type files, checks strict build-time validation, exercises consumer projections (subtractive / as_records), and verifies every discovered mark-type is reflected in mark_types/AGENTS.md.
observations: Imports MarkTypeRegistry, MarkType, _build_mark_type, and MARK_TYPE_FIELDS from runtime.mark_types.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and tallies passing checks.
Defines a raises(label, fn) helper that expects ValueError or TypeError.
Section 1 discovers mark-types and asserts seed ids (gold_likelihood, ai_fingerprint, contradiction) are present with expected shapes/directions.
Section 2 writes a temporary mark_types file to prove drop-in discovery, removes it, and rediscovers to prove un-registration.
Section 3 exercises strict validation: empty id, id/filename mismatch, missing/empty value_shape, unknown fields, non-dict declarations.
Section 4 calls reg.subtractive() and reg.as_records() to verify consumer-facing projections.
Section 5 reads mark_types/AGENTS.md and checks each id and MarkTypeRegistry are mentioned.
standouts: The explicit BAR claim: 'add-a-row = a file, no code edit' is tested by actually writing and deleting a file in the source tree.
FAIL LOUD validation suite treats malformed mark-types as build-time errors.
DRIFT HOME check ties runtime registry contents to human-readable AGENTS.md documentation.
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert and os.chdir to root the test.
Uses a global mutable PASS counter rather than a test framework.
Uses a temporary file inside the mark_types source directory for dynamic discovery testing.
Catches only ValueError/TypeError in raises, letting other exceptions fail the test directly.
concerns: Writing a temporary file into the source tree during acceptance testing could leave artifacts or race with other processes if cleanup fails.
Global PASS counter and side-effectful check() make the test harder to integrate with standard pytest runners.
os.chdir(ROOT) mutates global process state, which can affect other tests if run together.
notes: A self-contained acceptance script that proves mark-types are discovered from files, validated strictly, and kept in sync with AGENTS.md.
questions: What contract does runtime.mark_types enforce for value_shape and direction beyond the fields checked here?
How is MarkTypeRegistry.rediscover implemented relative to discover?
Is AGENTS.md expected to be the single source of human-readable truth for all mark-types?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to adversary-verify a file-discovered, dynamically extensible mark-type registry with validation and drift checks; the file does exactly that through concrete assertions and file I/O.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish that mark-types can be added, removed, and validated purely by dropping files into a directory, without editing registry code, while keeping documentation synchronized.
novelty: The test itself is framed as an 'adversary-verified BAR' with a numbered mechanism checklist, and it physically creates/deletes a source file to prove the no-code-edit claim rather than mocking the registry.
contribution: An acceptance harness that validates the discoverability, dynamic lifecycle, strict validation, consumer projections, and documentation alignment of the mark-type registry.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script for a file-discovered mark-type registry that validates discovery, dynamic registration, strict build-time validation, consumer projections, and AGENTS.md drift.
intention_for_embedding: To adversary-verify that mark-types are managed as file-discovered registry entries with no code edits required for additions and with documentation kept in sync.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition is truthy; on failure it raises with a FAIL label, and on success it increments the global PASS counter and prints a formatted ok message.

symbols.does: Helper that expects a callable to raise ValueError or TypeError and records the outcome via check.
symbols.description: raises(label, fn) invokes fn inside a try/except for ValueError and TypeError; if one of those exceptions is raised, it records a passing check, otherwise it records a failing check.
